titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 619

p.seg: Alexander by the grace of God king of the Scots to Robert of Bernham his mayor and provost of Berwick greeting.

We command you and enjoin, that you by no means hinder foreign merchants who come to the priory of Coldingham for buying the wool and other merchandise of the prior and convent of Coldingham, but that they should be able to buy and transport the said merchandise, reserving to ourselves our ancient customs.
As witnesses, Patrick earl of Dunbar, Walter Oliphant, justiciar of Lothian,
at Edinburgh, the 12th day of April, in the year of the reign of the lord king, the twenty-fourth [1238].
